items.summary: Oil prices saw a slight increase as investors awaited the meeting between U.S. President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with the Iran war a key focus. Despite U.S. sanctions, China remains a major buyer of Iranian oil, complicating efforts to resolve the conflict.

items.title: India bans sugar exports until September 2026 to cool local prices
items.summary: India has banned sugar exports until September 2026. This decision aims to manage domestic prices. The ban will impact global sugar markets, potentially benefiting Brazil and Thailand. Traders with existing contracts face challenges. Shipments already in the export pipeline will proceed under specific conditions. This move reflects concerns about domestic production lagging consumption.

items.title: Metal stocks shine as global base metal rally lifts investor sentiment
items.summary: Metal stocks surged on Tuesday, mirroring a sharp rally in global base metal prices driven by supply disruptions and robust demand. Gold loan firms also advanced following a government hike in customs duty on gold and silver, aimed at curbing imports and supporting domestic prices.

items.title: Margin revival, Semaglutide launch to drive Dr Reddy’s growth momentum in FY27
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Laboratories anticipates a gradual recovery this fiscal year. The company faced a profit drop last year due to US business pressures. However, operations in India, Europe, and emerging markets show healthy growth. New launches like Semaglutide and Abatacept are expected to drive future expansion. Margins are set to improve with better product mix and cost controls.

items.title: Sebi penalises Rashmi Saluja over alleged insider trading
items.summary: SEBI has fined former Religare Enterprises chairperson Rashmi Saluja ₹40 lakh for insider trading violations. She traded Religare shares while possessing unpublished price-sensitive information about the Burman Group's open offer. Saluja has also been ordered to return ₹1.9 crore in unlawful gains with interest.

items.title: Sebi proposes changes to municipal bond framework
items.summary: On Wednesday, the regulator has proposed allowing municipalities to explicitly raise bonds for refinancing existing debt, while mandating detailed disclosures on lenders, repayment schedules, interest costs and any past restructuring. It has also suggested capping the use of issue proceeds towards working capital requirements at 25% and said issue proceeds should not be used for general purposes.

items.title: Rupee may slide to 96-98 by December-end amid oil shock: Mint poll

items.summary: India is seen as a big growth market for global fast food chains that are serving customized menus and new strategies after years of mixed success here. The latest is Panda Express that is making an India entry as rising incomes, food delivery platforms and younger consumers reshape the landscape.

items.title: Govt mulls standardized fees for doctors, basic medical procedures
items.summary: India plans to regulate consultation fees and common procedure charges at private hospitals by amending the Clinical Establishments Act. The move aims to improve transparency and reduce healthcare costs, but doctors and hospitals warn against rigid price caps.

items.title: Rupee slides to a fresh low for third straight day on oil hit
items.summary: The Indian rupee hit a new all-time low for the third consecutive day, driven by soaring crude oil prices and increased dollar demand. Despite government efforts to curb gold imports, the rupee weakened significantly. Traders anticipate further volatility, with the Reserve Bank of India intervening to support the currency.

items.title: Trading volumes in unlisted shares plunge up to 70%
items.summary: Investor interest in unlisted shares is decreasing. Trading volumes have fallen significantly. This slowdown is due to lower prices and fewer new share offerings. Some popular companies still see activity. However, demand has softened in sectors like defence and hospitality. A stronger stock market is expected to boost this segment again.

items.title: RBI expected to hold rates in June despite rising inflation, H2 rate hike bets emerge
items.summary: Three out of twelve economists anticipate the Reserve Bank of India will raise policy rates this fiscal year due to inflation concerns, while others predict a prolonged pause. Most economists expect inflation to exceed RBI's forecast for FY27, with the bond market already pricing in tighter monetary policy.

items.title: India's gold rush meets a speeding ticket, but industry fears smuggling and job loss
items.summary: The finance ministry increased the total import duty on gold, silver and platinum from 9.18% to 18.45%. The new rate includes 10% basic customs duty, 5% agriculture cess and 3.45% Integrated GST, up from 5%, 1% and 3.18% before.

items.title: India working on ₹40,000 cr subsea gas pipeline project to help mitigate risks brought to fore by Hormuz blockade
items.summary: India is fast-tracking a deep-sea gas pipeline from Oman. This project aims to ensure steady gas supplies and reduce reliance on volatile LNG markets. The estimated ₹40,000 crore pipeline will connect Oman to Gujarat. It is expected to take five to seven years to build. This move addresses India's energy security concerns and its vulnerability to maritime choke points.

items.title: Airtel plans Rs 28,000 crore share swap: Deal with ICIL to raise parent’s stake in Airtel Africa & Mittal family’s stake in Airtel
items.summary: Bharti Airtel's board approved a ₹28,220 crore share-swap deal with ICIL, a Mittal family entity, to increase its stake in Airtel Africa to 79%. This cashless transaction preserves company cash, boosts ICIL's holding in Bharti Airtel, and is expected to be accretive to earnings per share.

items.summary: LIC Housing Finance announced a strong fourth quarter performance. Net profit rose 9.4% to ₹1,497 crore. This increase was driven by lower provisions for bad loans. The company's annual net profit also saw growth. A dividend of ₹10 per equity share has been recommended for FY26.

items.title: The big screen’s back in spotlight as Rs 13,000 crore box-office revival fades OTT boom
items.summary: Indian cinema is back to prioritizing movie theaters. Films now stream much later after their big screen debut. This shift is driven by studios seeking box-office success. Streaming platforms are spending less on movie rights. This change benefits all involved in the industry. Box-office collections reached a record high in 2025.

items.summary: Shivalik has received Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) approval for this acquisition while it has sought clearance from the Registrar of Companies. The deal comprises share swaps and a cash payment, people familiar with the matter said.

Producer prices surged, reinforcing expectations of prolonged Fed tightening. Markets also tracked Trump-Xi talks and rising geopolitical risks, while Morgan Stanley lifted its S&P target.

items.title: Why India’s generation adequacy plan needs a clear counterfactual
items.summary: As India imports 25-30% of its coal, fuel price risk over a 20-25 year asset life cannot be ignored. In contrast, renewable energy generation reduces exposure to recurring fuel supply risks and shifts the cost structure toward upfront investment and system integration

items.title: Tata group’s Impetis out-licenses arthritis and autoimmune drugs to Vyome
items.summary: In a significant move for medical innovation, Impetis Biosciences, a subsidiary of Tata group, has teamed up with Vyome Holdings from the US to license two cutting-edge experimental treatments. Focusing on challenges like rheumatoid arthritis and hair thinning, these selective JAK inhibitors are engineered to mitigate risks tied to earlier treatments.

items.title: Zydus Lifesciences acquires US pharma firm Assertio for $166 million
items.summary: In a bold move that positions Zydus at the forefront of the U.S. specialty oncology market, the Indian drug manufacturer has secured a $166.40 million deal to acquire Assertio Holdings. The purchase includes Assertio’s essential cancer treatment, Rolvedon, and offers Zydus a rich commercial network to elevate its oncology initiatives.

items.title: Fuel prices likely to rise if war continues: RBI Guv
items.summary: "If this is to continue for longer period of time, it is just a matter of time before the government will pass on some of the price increases," Malhotra said at a conference hosted by the Swiss National Bank and the International Monetary Fund in Switzerland on Tuesday. Excise duties had been cut while state-run fuel retailers were absorbing the increase in crude prices as the conflict continues, he added.

items.title: India holds inter-ministerial meeting to speed up battery storage and grid upgrades
items.summary: During a crucial summit, leaders examined effective pathways to accelerate the deployment of battery storage technologies. The dialogue included vital topics such as enhancing transmission capacities and the potential of variable time-of-day pricing. These approaches are strategically aimed at amplifying the adoption of renewable energy and reinforcing the stability of the power grid.

items.title: Duty hike may bring out more old gold into market
items.summary: Industry executives said promoting sales of lower carat jewellery like 14K and 9K can reduce imports by 20-30%, and discouraging investments in gold bars and coins can reduce imports by another 20% -30%. India annually imports 750-800 tonnes of gold to meet its demand. Revamping the Gold Monetisation Scheme can also help the government to tap 25,000 tonnes of grandfather stock.

items.title: Oil settles lower on US rate hike fears; investors watch Trump-Xi meeting
items.summary: GLOBAL-OIL:Oil settles lower on US rate hike fears; investors watch Trump-Xi meeting

items.title: Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh top logistics performance ranking, Mizoram and Delhi also lead
items.summary: The LEADS 2025 report has unveiled the logistics champions of India, with Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Mizoram, and Delhi being hailed as 'exemplars'. Gujarat and Kerala are also making waves as 'high performers' among an elite group of 11 states, reflecting their exceptional efforts in logistics development.

items.title: Austerity drive: Duty hike pushes up bullion prices by 7%
items.summary: The government late Tuesday increased import duties on gold and silver to 15% from 6% with effect from May 13. The step is aimed at curbing overseas purchases of precious metals and reducing pressure on the country's foreign exchange reserves. The revised structure includes a 10% basic customs duty along with a 5% Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess (AIDC), lifting the effective import tax to 15%.

items.title: SpiceJet seeks Supreme Court approval to submit land title as security
items.summary: SpiceJet has challenged the Delhi High Court's May 4 and March 18 orders rejecting its request to furnish an unencumbered plot of land in Gurugram as security. The HC had directed the airline to immediately comply with its earlier order to deposit the amount and imposed a cost of ₹50,000 for repeatedly seeking modification of the deposit direction.

items.title: ATS Nobility Estates exits insolvency
items.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has permitted the withdrawal of insolvency proceedings against ATS Group's Nobility Estates. This decision brings relief to over 600 homebuyers of the Le Grandiose project. The committee of creditors approved a settlement with the erstwhile management. A significant amount will be paid, and debt to JM Financial will be settled through project units.

items.title: India targets $1 trillion exports in FY27 as FTAs come into effect, says Goyal
items.summary: With an eye on reaching $1 trillion in exports by FY27, India is launching into a new era of trade. The upcoming implementation of various free trade agreements, combined with efforts to streamline business regulations, is designed to create a robust trade environment.

items.title: Fertiliser subsidy may surpass  budgeted estimates by Rs 70k cr
items.summary: India imports over 80% of its DAP requirements and for urea domestic production covers only 30-35% of total demand. India is among top fertiliser importing countries in the world. The official said the Centre is committed to ensure that there are sufficient fertiliser stocks, and available at affordable rates to farmers.

items.title: Hormuz shutdown deepens oil crisis; IEA warns of record inventory drop, demand plunge
items.summary: Global oil stocks are depleting rapidly. The Iran war is worsening supply disruptions. This situation will continue for months. The market faces a severe shortage until October. Oil prices are under pressure. Demand is also falling sharply. This is the steepest drop since the Covid pandemic. Some signs suggest the drawdown may be slowing.

items.title: Aditya Birla group, KKR left in race for Sprng Energy
items.summary: Aditya Birla Group and KKR are vying to buy Shell's renewable energy firm Sprng Energy. Binding financial bids are in, and Shell will negotiate with the top two contenders next week. India's National Investment and Infrastructure Fund and Actis are out of the running. The deal could be worth around $2 billion. Shell is reviewing strategic options for Sprng Energy.

items.title: Govt turns up heat on induction push, makers asked to ramp up production
items.summary: In a bold push towards sustainability, the government is calling on manufacturers to ramp up induction cooktop production. With plans to acquire millions of units in the coming year and a half, this initiative signals a significant shift from LPG cooking methods.

items.title: Silicon Valley Prosecutor Targets Foreign Corporate Spying
items.summary: Silicon Valley’s top federal prosecutor said his top priorities include cases of trade-secret theft and economic espionage involving foreign adversaries such as China.

items.title: Morgan Stanley lifts India FY27 GDP forecast to 6.7% on demand strength
items.summary: Morgan Stanley estimates a promising economic growth rate of 6.7% for India in FY27, driven by vibrant domestic consumption, extensive infrastructure investments by the government, and a surge in services exports. Although there are global uncertainties and climbing oil prices, the economy is expected to bounce back. Inflation might hover at an average of 4.

items.title: Centre raises MSP for 14 kharif crops, boosts support for pulses and oilseeds
items.summary: Based on expected margins over cost of production, the MSP hike is the most for moong at 61%, followed by bajra and maize (56% each) and tur (54%). For other crops, it is estimated to be around 50%. The MSP for paddy (rice), the main kharif crop, has been increased by Rs 72 per quintal, taking it to Rs 2,441 for the 'common' variety and Rs 2,461 for 'Grade A'.

items.title: Warsh clinches Senate approval to be Fed's next chair as inflation intensifies
items.summary: In a pivotal moment for the economy, the US Senate has officially confirmed Kevin Warsh as the chair of the Federal Reserve, just as inflation escalates. With President Donald Trump advocating for reduced interest rates, Warsh's leadership will be crucial at the next central bank meeting, where policymakers are wrestling with the implications of rising prices.

items.title: Big auto bets big: Carmakers line up Rs 40,000-crore capex push amid demand boom
items.summary: While Maruti Suzuki has earmarked record investments of Rs 14,000 crore in FY27 to add annual capacity for 500,000 cars, Hyundai Motor India has budgeted for an all-time high capex of Rs 7,500 crore for introducing two new SUVs and ramp up production at its new facility in Talegaon (Maharashtra).

items.title: New steel policy in works as India shifts focus to premium and green steel
items.summary: India is developing an innovative steel policy aimed at redefining the industry landscape by 2035 and 2047. This initiative will establish aggressive targets for green, specialty, and stainless steel production, emphasizing high-quality products while striving to minimize import dependence.

items.title: Siemens eyes exit from cancer care chain American Oncology Institute
items.summary: Siemens Healthineers is exiting its Indian cancer care chain, American Oncology Institute. The deal is valued between ₹1,500-2,000 crore. Private equity funds and Healthcare Global Enterprises are potential bidders. AOI operates 16 hospitals in India and one in Sri Lanka. This move aligns with Siemens Healthineers' broader strategic reviews. The Indian cancer care market is experiencing significant growth and investment.

items.title: Eli Lilly brings monthly Alzheimer's therapy donanemab to India at ₹91,688 per vial
items.summary: Eli Lilly is making Alzheimer's a major focus for its future innovations. The company is also expanding its specialty medicine offerings in India. Eli Lilly has launched Lormalzi, a new monthly treatment for early Alzheimer's disease. This drug targets amyloid plaques. It will be available in India this month. The treatment costs 91,688 rupees per vial.

items.title: Coal Gasification Scheme worth Rs 37.5k cr cleared to fortify energy security
items.summary: Financial support to developers will be provided at a maximum of 20% of the cost of plant and machinery through a competitive bidding process, where incentives will be disbursed in four equal instalments linked to project milestones. The incentive for a project will be capped at Rs 5,000 crore and for any single product, except synthetic natural gas and urea, will be capped at Rs 9,000 crore. Incentive for a single entity group will be capped at Rs 12,000 crore across all projects.

items.title: Weight-loss drug semaglutide set to get cheaper as generic wave builds in India
items.summary: Prices for generic semaglutide, a popular weight-loss drug, are expected to fall by more than half in the coming months. This sharp decline is anticipated with the entry of smaller brands and a significant drop in raw material costs. The cost for a month's dose could reach as low as ₹500.

items.title: From oncology to obesity, Pfizer bets big on India for its next growth wave
items.summary: Pfizer is focusing on India for future growth. The company plans to launch new medicines quickly and at affordable prices. India is seen as a crucial market for new treatments in cancer, obesity, and vaccines. Pfizer is also working on local clinical studies for future drug launches.

items.title: Drug regulator drafts guidelines on high-risk solvents after tainted syrup deaths in kids
items.summary: India's drug regulator is creating new rules for dangerous solvents in children's medicines. This follows tragic deaths linked to contaminated cough syrups. Experts found these solvents can contain toxic impurities. The regulator is consulting with companies to assess current practices and explore safer alternatives. This action is vital after several children died from Indian-made cough syrups.

items.title: Learn from Sri Lanka’s experience on impact of fertilizer supply chains: experts
items.summary: The experts participating in a panel discussion on the topic ‘Impact of West Asia Crisis on Economic Disruptions and Sustainability: Implications for India’ say India should continue to follow the current policies while focussing on a transition to sustainable practices in the long term

items.title: Fed is 'dead serious' about getting inflation down, Kashkari says
items.summary: In a recent address, Neel Kashkari, the Minneapolis Federal Reserve President, shared a cautiously positive outlook on the labor market in the U.S. He highlighted how the current Iran conflict is a significant driver of inflationary pressures. As such, the possibility of further interest rate increases remains a viable option.

items.title: Dr. Reddy’s Q4 dips 86% on lower sales, multiple one offs to ₹221 crore
items.summary: Total revenue from operations of the Hyderabad-headquartered generic drugmaker was lower at ₹7,546.4 crore (₹8,528.4 crore), according to the results prepared as per Indian Accounting Standards (Ind AS)

items.title: Noida airport to charge 4x higher user development fees than Delhi’s IGI airport: Aera
items.summary: The tariff at Noida airport is less than what it had sought. The regulator hasn't yet decided on user fees for Navi Mumbai International Airport.

items.title: Gem and Jewellery Council urges jewellery trades to stay calm amid gold duty hike
items.summary: "Business is now going to become difficult on the back of the PM Modi's austerity measures and following the import duty hike in bullion," GJC chairman said

items.title: Gold imports from Dubai may rise after duty hike: GTRI
items.summary: India's recent hike in import duties on gold and silver opens the door for enhanced trade relations with Dubai. Thanks to the India-UAE trade agreement, gold coming from Dubai will incur a more favorable 14 percent duty, a welcome change from the anticipated 15 percent. This scenario creates an exciting opportunity for silver imports as well.

items.title: Jewellery stocks take Rs 60,000 crore hit in 3 days on govt's double-blow. Is the worst behind?
items.summary: Jewellery stocks have plunged up to 20% in three sessions, erasing nearly Rs 60,000 crore in investor wealth after PM Narendra Modi urged households to defer gold purchases and the government hiked import duties. The double blow has rattled sentiment, though analysts see no structural threat to India’s long-term gold demand.

items.title: India wheat procurement tops 30-million tonne mark, heading towards target of 34.5 mt
items.summary: Purchases surpass year-ago levels for first time this season despite Madhya Pradesh lag, with Punjab and Haryana showing strong gains

items.title: Cipla Q4 Results: Profit falls 55% YoY to Rs 555 crore; co declares Rs 13/sh dividend
items.summary: Cipla Ltd announced a significant 55% year-on-year drop in its consolidated net profit for the fourth quarter, reaching Rs 555 crore. This contrasts with Rs 1,222 crore reported in the same period last year. The company's Board has recommended a final dividend of Rs 13 per share for the fiscal year ending March 2026.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki crosses 30-lakh car dispatches through railways
items.summary: MSIL says rail-based vehicle dispatches now account for 26.5% of total outbound logistics, marking a major milestone in its green logistics push.

items.title: Government doubles import duty on gold & silver, effective rate now at 18.4%
items.summary: Industry players and experts warn that this decision will not only encourage a shift to smuggling but will also have other negative effects on employment. The government has not yet released an official statement on the duty hikes

items.title: The 2013 ghost returns: Will higher gold duties actually restrict imports or just fuel the grey market?
items.summary: India’s shock hike in gold and silver import duty to 15% has revived memories of the 2013 crisis. While the move aims to curb dollar outflows and stabilise the rupee, experts warn it may simply push more demand into the grey market, with limited impact on India’s structural gold appetite.

items.title: MSCI May rejig: Four stocks added, four excluded from  Global Standard Index
items.summary: Federal Bank, Multi Commodity Exchange of India, National Aluminium and Indian Bank will enter the ‌index, while Hyundai Motor India, Jubilant Foodworks, Kalyan Jewellers and Rail Vikas Nigam will ‌be excluded.

items.title: Jaishankar meets Chilean FM Mackenna; discusses trade diversification, multilateral cooperation
items.summary: India and Chile are boosting economic ties. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met his Chilean counterpart to enhance cooperation. Discussions focused on diversifying markets and promoting exports. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal also reviewed progress on a Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement. This pact aims to expand trade and investment between the two nations.

items.title: India vegetable oil imports rise 13% to 7.94 million tonnes in Apr-Nov period
items.summary: Palm oil imports nearly doubled, while soft oils like soybean and sunflower declined. In value terms, imports jumped 19% to Rs 87,000 crore

items.title: Marked increase in regulatory burden on food processing sector: Teamlease Regtech founder
items.summary: Stringent food safety norms have meant tighter packaging and labelling requirements, higher traceability expectations, environmental regulations and consumer protection standards

items.title: SpiceJet moves Supreme Court against  ₹144.5 crore deposit order in Maran dispute
items.summary: The airline has challenged a Delhi High Court directive requiring an immediate deposit in its long-running arbitration battle with Kalanithi Maran and KAL Airways, citing mounting financial stress and disruptions linked to the West Asia conflict.

items.title: India’s emergence has a deficit that may not be too late for us to collectively close—empathy
items.summary: The gaping divide between the rich and poor in India is stark, especially in its cities like Bengaluru. This cannot be blamed solely on policy. What individuals see and choose not to see could make a significant difference to how millions of lives are lived.

items.title: GJEPC says higher gold import duty may raise prices, fuel smuggling and hurt MSME exporters
items.summary: While backing Narendra Modi’s broader call for reducing non-essential imports and supporting the economy, the industry body said higher duties have rarely reduced gold imports in proportion to rising prices.

items.title: Germany wants to put TikTok 'in European hands'
items.summary: The EU told TikTok in February that it needed to change its "addictive design" or risk heavy fines

items.title: RBI Governor sees fuel price hike if crude oil stays high
items.summary: His remarks came after Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged voluntary austerity, including cutting down on petrol and diesel use and putting off gold purchases, to preserve foreign exchange reserves.

items.title: Gold, silver futures rally 7% after govt raises import duty on bullion
items.summary: The duty hike could moderate imports in the near term as the government looks to contain pressure on the current account deficit

items.title: Smaller Indian airlines cut international flights sharply amid West Asia war
items.summary: Air India Express, Akasa Air and SpiceJet have cut overseas departures by nearly 60% as Gulf route exposure and operational pressures weigh on smaller carriers, even as IndiGo and Air India see smaller reductions.

items.title: Gold prices surge in Hyderabad
items.summary: Gold rates jump by ₹800 per gram in the city as the government raises customs duty; one gram now costs ₹16,500

items.title: HPCL Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 46% YoY to Rs 4,902 crore; Rs 19.25/sh dividend declared
items.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) reported a significant 46% surge in standalone net profit to Rs 4,902 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26. The oil marketing giant also announced a final dividend of Rs 19.25 per share.  Standalone total income saw a 4.5% year-on-year increase to Rs 1.24 lakh crore during the same period.

items.title: Why gold’s Rs 10,000 spike after import duty increase is a one-off move
items.summary: Gold and silver prices staged a sharp comeback on Wednesday, with MCX futures for both metals surging nearly 6% in a single session after the government raised customs duty on precious metal imports. Gold prices jumped about Rs 9,600, while silver soared nearly Rs 17,000, marking one of the biggest one-day rallies in recent months amid lingering volatility from Middle East tensions and tariff uncertainty.

items.title: Missing lens: markets must put a price tag on the climate risks that companies face
items.summary: Indian markets react instantly to oil shocks, elections and earnings misses. Yet climate risk, one of the biggest long-term threats to corporate performance, barely affects how companies and assets are valued. This disconnect can’t survive contact with reality.

items.title: Smallcaps rally! IIFL Finance, Hindustan Copper, Meesho, other stocks jump up to 7%
items.summary: Smallcap stocks, including IIFL Finance, Meesho, and GRSE, surged up to 7% on Wednesday, outperforming benchmark indices. This rally occurred despite broader market caution, with the Nifty Smallcap 100 index showing robust gains. Experts remain optimistic about mid and smallcap earnings, viewing recent corrections as opportunities.

items.title: Sugar Cosmetics seeks  ₹100-150 crore rescue funding at steep valuation cut
items.summary: The beauty brand is seeking fresh funding from family offices, HNIs and existing investors as it battles a cash crunch and retraces its offline expansion.

items.title: Sensex to hit 89,000? Why Morgan Stanley is betting big on India Inc after 6-quarter slowdown
items.summary: Morgan Stanley has turned more bullish on India, forecasting the BSE Sensex could rise to 89,000 by June 2027, implying around 15% upside from current levels. Strategist Ridham Desai said India is emerging from a six-quarter earnings slowdown into a stronger growth phase after a “mid-cycle” pause.

items.title: Rupee recovers 16 paise from all-time low to 95.52 against U.S. dollar in early trade
items.summary: Meanwhile, the dollar index, which gauges the greenback's strength against a basket of six currencies, was trading at 98.30, up 0.01%

items.title: Groww shares jump 6% even as Peak XV, two other US investors sell stake worth Rs 5,326 crore. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures shares rebounded on Wednesday, recovering Tuesday’s losses triggered by a Rs 5,326 crore stake sale by Peak XV Partners, Ribbit Capital, and YC Holdings. The investors sold over 29.5 crore shares via bulk deals at an average price of Rs 180.43 apiece, a 1.5% discount to the previous closing price.

items.title: Malabar Gold & Diamonds seeks measures to support Gold Monetisation Scheme
items.summary: Company emphasises focus on recycling, exchange, re-use, and monetisation of existing domestic gold to reduce import dependency

items.title: Maruti Suzuki ramps up green logistics, targets 35% rail dispatch by 2031
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki has already increased rail share to 26.5%, crossing 30 lakh cumulative rail dispatches

items.title: Berger Paints shares soar over 9% after Q4 net profit jumps 27% to Rs 335 crore
items.summary: Berger Paints India saw its shares surge following a robust Q4 FY26 profit jump of over 27%, driven by strong sales volumes and better margins. Despite global uncertainties, the company anticipates continued growth fueled by construction chemicals and new product launches, with price adjustments expected to bolster profitability.

items.title: Japan megabanks to gain access to Anthropic's Mythos in about two weeks, source says
items.summary: Mythos is viewed by ⁠cybersecurity experts as posing significant challenges to the banking industry and its legacy technology systems

items.title: Fuel price pass-through needs monitoring as weather risks also posing pressure on inflation: BoB report
items.summary: Rising fuel prices and weather uncertainties could push India's inflation higher in FY27, despite current low CPI levels. Bank of Baroda warns that global energy costs impacting food prices, coupled with domestic weather challenges like heatwaves and El Nino, necessitate close monitoring of food inflation. Core inflation remains stable, but vigilance is advised.

items.title: Ventive Hospitality Q4 profit climbs 72% to Rs 259 crore, revenue up 21%
items.summary: Ventive Hospitality Ltd announced strong financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Net profit surged 72% in the fourth quarter and full-year profit reached Rs 502 crore. Revenue also saw substantial increases. The company's hotel sector and annuity portfolio performed well. Strategic acquisitions were also completed during the period, positioning Ventive for future growth.

items.title: Streaming industry shifts to consolidation mode amid rising costs, competition and clutter
items.summary: The streaming industry is consolidating as smaller players face rising costs and competition. Experts suggest these moves indicate a shift towards strategic alliances and efficiency in content monetization and distribution.

items.title: Stock markets pare early gains; trade lower
items.summary: From the 30-Sensex firms, Power Grid, NTPC, Bajaj Finance, State Bank of India, Titan and Axis Bank were among the biggest laggards

items.title: Black Box completes acquisition of Brazilian company 2S Inovações Tecnológicas S.A.
items.summary: The acquisition, effective May 1, strengthens Black Box’s capabilities and market presence across Latin America, particularly in areas such as data center networking, digital connectivity, cybersecurity, and managed infrastructure

items.title: Hindustan Zinc shares surge 5% as silver prices near Rs 3 lakh/kg after import duty hike; Vedanta shares jump 4%
items.summary: Government has raised import duties on gold and silver to 15 percent. This move has caused precious metal prices to surge. Hindustan Zinc and Vedanta shares saw significant jumps following the announcement. The government aims to curb imports and stabilize the rupee. Hindustan Zinc, India's largest silver producer, is expected to benefit from higher silver prices.

items.title: Gold rallies past ₹1.63 lakh amid global uncertainty, weaker rupee
items.summary: The rally in domestic bullion was further amplified after the Indian government raised the total import duty on gold and silver to 15% from 6%

items.title: West Asia crisis yet to impact inflation, but fuel prices pass-through to shape outlook: Report
items.summary: India's retail inflation remains stable despite global tensions. Future price trends will depend on fuel price pass-through and summer crop coverage. Imported inflation has slightly eased. Restaurants and accommodation saw price rises due to LPG shortages. Softening gold and silver prices helped ease personal care inflation. Retail inflation is under control for now.

items.title: RBI likely to maintain rate pause as FY27 CPI seen at 4.8-4.9%: Report
items.summary: Elara Capital report flags upside risks from oil prices and rupee depreciation, with potential hike only if inflation hits 6 per cent durably

items.title: AERA defers 15% revenue recovery to moderate Noida airport charges
items.summary: Regulator says part of airport’s revenue requirement was shifted to next tariff cycle to reduce burden on passengers and airlines

items.title: Vodafone Idea shares surge 30% in a month. Should you buy or wait for consolidation?
items.summary: Vodafone Idea shares have surged 30% in a month, driven by a reduction in AGR dues and the return of Kumar Mangalam Birla as chairman. Recent reports of Vodafone Plc considering share transfer also boosted the stock. Analysts suggest waiting for consolidation due to the sharp rally, with high-risk investors eyeing a turnaround play.

items.title: Crypto regulation enters a new era with US Clarity Act
items.summary: The proposed U.S. Clarity Act seeks to establish a comprehensive regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, DeFi and tokenised assets. The legislation aims to improve investor protection, clarify oversight responsibilities and support blockchain innovation, while introducing stricter compliance standards that could significantly reshape the future of digital asset markets.

items.title: SBI shares extend fall to 20% from peak after Q4 NIMs contraction rattles investors. What's ahead for investors?
items.summary: SBI shares have fallen over 20% from their peak after Q4 earnings revealed margin contraction and a sequential drop in net interest income. Despite this, brokerages maintain a positive outlook, citing strong fundamentals and recommending a 'Buy' rating with revised price targets.

items.title: Neuland Laboratories shares rally 7% as profit jumps seven-fold and revenue doubles in Q4
items.summary: Neuland Laboratories shares saw a significant jump following a strong Q4 performance. The company reported a massive increase in profit and revenue year-on-year. This robust financial result was driven by strong business momentum across key segments. The company is also investing in future growth through strategic initiatives.

items.title: Noida airport domestic flyers to pay ₹490 user development fee: AERA
items.summary: Tariff for greenfield facility to rise gradually till FY31 with UDF up to ₹1,461 for departing international passengers

items.title: TVS Credit Q4 net profit up 13% at ₹256 crore
items.summary: For the full year FY26, net profit after tax stood at ₹913 crore, a growth of 19% from FY25

items.title: Gold duty under India-UAE pact raised in line with standard rate hike
items.summary: Gold import under specified quota to now attract duties of 14 per cent instead of 5 per cent; experts advise tighter origin rules to check misuse

items.title: HFCL, Ideaforge, among 10 smallcap stocks that more than doubled in the first 25 sessions of FY27​
items.summary: ​Several smallcap stocks, including HFCL and Ideaforge, have more than doubled in the initial 25 trading sessions of FY27, significantly outperforming the benchmark Sensex's 7% rise. This rally occurred amidst market recovery following a March selloff, with companies like CIAN Agro Industries and Indo Tech Transformers showing substantial gains.

items.title: MSCI decides against adding Adani Energy to global standard indexes in May review
items.summary: MSCI has excluded Adani Energy Solutions from inclusion in its indexes during the May 2026 review after the stock was placed on an “Ineligible Alert Board” due to India’s ASM surveillance framework. MSCI said securities under the Short-Term or Long-Term Additional Surveillance Measure (ASM) will not be added to its Investable Market Indexes during periodic reviews.

items.title: Texmaco Rail & Engineering shares zoom 13% on strong Q4 show, order win worth Rs 4,045 crore
items.summary: Texmaco Rail & Engineering shares rose sharply after reporting a 45% rise in Q4 net profit. Despite lower revenue, margins improved, while a Rs 4,045 crore South Africa order win and defence foray plans boosted sentiment. The stock hit an intraday high amid strong growth and a global expansion outlook.

items.title: Crude oil futures decline as markets track West Asia ceasefire, Trump’s China visit
items.summary: According to a Short-Term Energy Outlook global oil inventories will decrease by 2.6 million barrels per day this year

items.title: Cohance Lifesciences shares slip 7% after Jefferies cuts target price, downgrades on weak Q4
items.summary: Cohance Lifesciences shares dropped significantly after reporting an 84% year-on-year decline in March-quarter net profit. Jefferies downgraded the stock to "Underperform" citing management instability and weak visibility, while Goldman Sachs maintained its "Buy" rating, seeing long-term opportunities despite a challenging near-term outlook.

items.title: JBM Auto doubles down on electric vehicles push in strategic shift from auto components manufacture
items.summary: Board renames OEM Division as ‘EV Business’ as receivables jump 117%, cash flow drops 59%, and debt rises 61% amid rapid electric bus expansion

items.title: Multibagger MTAR Technologies shares rally 10% after raising FY27 guidance; Q4 net profit surges 223% YoY
items.summary: MTAR Technologies shares surged after the company reported a significant 223% jump in fourth-quarter net profit, driven by robust revenue growth of 67%. The precision engineering firm also raised its FY27 revenue growth guidance to over 80%, citing capacity expansions and strong order books across clean energy, nuclear, and aerospace sectors.

items.title: Rupee recovers 16 paise from all-time low to 95.52 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: Forex traders said market participants are expecting some resilience in the dollar-rupee pair as gold importers curb their demand.

items.title: Gold has evolved beyond its traditional role—which may help us mobilize holdings for financial resilience
items.summary: In much of India, gold is an integral part of the household financial playbook. But the cultural context has been evolving in ways that make it easier to deploy vast private holdings—among the world’s biggest—for productive economic purposes.

items.title: Jio Platforms appoints Akash Ambani as managing director ahead of IPO
items.summary: In a key pre-IPO move, Jio Platforms has appointed Akash Ambani as managing director for five years, as the company moves closer to filing its DRHP with Sebi for a blockbuster listing.

items.title: Nestlé India launches Nespresso store in Mumbai
items.summary: Third outlet for the brand focuses on personalised coffee discovery and experience in Bandra Kurla Complex

items.title: Nifty slips for 4th day; Crude above $100, Rupee at 95.63 weigh on markets
items.summary: The rupee has touched a fresh low of 95.63 against the dollar, with USD/INR hovering in the 95.6–95.7 range amid elevated crude prices, firm dollar demand, and sustained foreign institutional outflows

items.title: Rising inflation, Hormuz deadlock, Trump-Xi meet: What’s driving global news headlines
items.summary: Inflation is flaring up again as the West Asia war disrupts energy flows and keeps oil above $100 amid a prolonged Strait of Hormuz standoff. The Trump-Xi meeting could reshape trade dynamics, while the 2026 World Cup promises a sizeable global growth boost.

items.title: India's energy illusion: why ambitious targets mean nothing without a coherent plan
items.summary: Soaring crude oil prices triggered by the Israel-US-Iran war are straining India's economy, exposing its heavy dependence on imported fossil fuels and a fragile energy strategy.&nbsp;

items.title: India's travel industry braces for hit from Modi's appeal to avoid foreign trips
items.summary: The travel pullback is unfolding against a backdrop of rising global inflation, with crude oil trading above $100 a barrel after Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz choked off a fifth of global supply of oil ​and liquefied natural gas

items.title: How the US-Israel conflict with Iran is exposing cracks in the petrodollar system
items.summary: Rising geopolitical tensions, global fragmentation and efforts by emerging economies to diversify away from the US dollar are also accelerating concerns over the long-term stability of the US-centred financial order

items.title: Oil prices ease after rising in three consecutive sessions, Brent still around $107/bbl
items.summary: Crude prices pause after an 8% surge, but the 75-day closure of the Strait of Hormuz keeps oil above $100, squeezing India and forcing tough choices as OMC losses swell.

items.title: India-Japan seek to advance coop in critical minerals, semiconductors & ICT
items.summary: India and Japan have strengthened economic ties. A key dialogue focused on industrial and technological cooperation. Five sectors were prioritised: critical minerals, semiconductors, ICT, clean energy, and pharmaceuticals. Both nations aim to build resilient supply chains. Discussions also covered defense, trade, and energy security.

items.title: India raises gold and silver tariffs to 15% to ease pressure on forex reserves
items.summary: The revised duty structure includes a 10 per cent basic customs duty and a 5 per cent Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess

items.title: Global sugar deficit expected in 2026-27 as demand outpaces production
items.summary: Sugar output is projected to fall sharply in the European Union, the UK and Thailand, where low sugar prices are prompting farmers to switch to other crops such as cassava

items.title: Diaspora dollars: be thankful for non-residents sending money home but let’s not taken these flows for granted
items.summary: With India’s foreign-exchange reserves at $691 billion, we can breathe easy on the external front for now. Credit the Indian diaspora for some of this. But resident citizens, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged, must do their bit as well.

items.title: India’s economic strength defies foreign outflow fears, S&P Says
items.summary: S&P Global Ratings said India is coping with global financial pressures better than headline data indicate, adding that concerns over foreign investment outflows are overstated. The agency said India has enough buffers to manage a wider current-account deficit caused by rising oil prices.

items.title: Buzzing stocks: Jewellers, Texmaco Rail, Interarch, Active Clothing, Choice Intl, Puravankara in focus
items.summary: Jewellery stocks may remain under pressure after the government raised gold and silver import duties to 15%, while companies including Texmaco Rail, Interarch Building Solutions, Alfa Transformers and Puravankara announced major order wins, investments and strategic expansion plans

items.title: Mint Quick Edit | India’s inflation incline could put rate hikes back in RBI’s policy consideration set
items.summary: India’s inflation may still be below the Reserve Bank of India’s 4% target, but is on an upward trajectory. Unless price pressures somehow ease, tighter monetary policy may only be a matter of time.

items.title: India books phosphate fertilizer at 40% above pre-war prices
items.summary: India has contracted diammonium phosphate (DAP) fertilizer at prices nearly 40% higher than pre-war levels as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and lifts global prices. Indian Potash Ltd. agreed to buy about 1.35 million tons of DAP for delivery on the west and east coasts at around $930-$935 per ton, exceeding the 1.2 million tons sought in last week’s tender.

items.title: India raises gold, silver import tariffs to 15%; revises customs duty on precious metal findings
items.summary: The Indian Government has raised effective import tariffs on gold and silver to 15%. The move aims to curb bullion imports and strengthen the Indian National Rupee against the US Dollar

items.title: India raises gold and silver tariffs to 15% to curb imports and support rupee
items.summary: The government imposed a 10% basic customs duty along with a 5% Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess

items.title: Blackstone-backed Ventive doubles down on luxury hospitality with  ₹2,000 cr push
items.summary: With a  ₹2,000 crore expansion, Ventive Hospitality aims to capitalize on luxury travel demand. The firm operates 13 assets and plans to grow by focusing on large luxury projects and acquisitions in key markets like Goa, Sri Lanka and the Maldives.

items.title: Aditya Birla Health bets on wellness incentives to improve claims ratios
items.summary: Aditya Birla Health wants to reward healthier customers, nudge those on the edge to be more consistent, and, lastly, intervene with high-risk customers to help them manage their health.

items.title: A third legal salvo against Tata Trusts ahead of key 16 May meeting
items.summary: A legal notice has been sent to the trustees of Sir Ratan Tata Trust regarding the transfer of shares in Tata Sons from a small trust to Naval Tata, father of Noel Tata and the late Ratan Tata.

items.title: Auto Inc stares at stress in Bharat as signs of slowdown emerge
items.summary: India's largest tractor makers and a motorcycle company anticipate declining sales due to poor monsoon conditions, risking stress in the rural auto industry. Mahindra forecasts 5% growth, while Escorts expects a 2-3% decline.&nbsp;

items.title: Video pitch, assessment, socials scan—hiring goes beyond CVs
items.summary: The CV is clearly losing its position as the main document that decides who moves forward. Hiring teams now lean more on assessments, video introductions and digital footprints to judge skills, communication and workplace fit.

items.title: Actis restarts Nxt-Infra sale process, appoints Ambit
items.summary: Interest in road assets has been buoyant in India lately, driven by the availability of a large number of assets that attract large institutional investors, including pension and sovereign wealth funds.

items.title: Inflation in driver’s seat likely to stall discretionary spend
items.summary: While consumer demand had started improving over the past four months after a prolonged slowdown, rising crude oil prices, supply disruptions and Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent call for austerity could threaten to derail that recovery, said executives and analysts. The impact is expected to be sharpest in electronics.

items.title: Containing war impact on growth, current account: PMO stitching up plan to boost foreign fund flow
items.summary: Officials at both the finance ministry and Niti Aayog are identifying economic opportunities presented by the Iran conflict, as well as gauging the impact of the surge in global oil prices on the economy under different price bands. Niti Aayog will likely draw up a report on matter.

items.title: Tata Power Q4 PAT up 8%, revenue down 8%, dividend at ₹2.50 per share
items.summary: Praveer Sinha, CEO and Managing Director, Tata Power, said, “This quarter and year reflect our sustained focus on creating long-term value through disciplined growth, operational excellence and strategic partnerships”

items.title: GDP data release revised to June 7 each year
items.summary: The statistics ministry has adjusted the release date for provisional GDP estimates and fourth-quarter data to June 7 annually, moving from the last working day of May. This change, driven by data availability delays, will see FY26 provisional GDP and Q4FY26 data released on June 5.

items.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
items.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

items.title: Govt eyes new employment blueprint after labour code rollout
items.summary: The central government has fully operationalised four new labour codes, consolidating 29 laws into a simplified framework designed to be both pro-worker and business-friendly. These codes aim to foster effective coordination between labour and industry. The ministry also plans a comprehensive employment policy to guide future job generation.

items.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
items.summary: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

items.title: CEA Nageswaran says West Asia crisis a balance of payments stress test; to impact CAD, inflation & exchange rate
items.summary: Large companies must pay micro and small enterprises on time. This eases their working capital and reduces funding costs. Managing the current account, financing it, and preventing currency depreciation are key macroeconomic goals for FY27. The West Asia crisis presents a balance of payments stress test.

items.title: West Asia crisis a wake-up call for self-reliance: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
items.summary: The West Asia crisis serves as a crucial reminder for Indian industries to boost self-reliance and efficiency. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged businesses to reduce import dependency and focus on indigenous design and manufacturing. He also highlighted the need for faster electric vehicle adoption to cut oil bills.

items.title: India launches new IFAD-backed programme to boost rural incomes and climate resilience
items.summary: Aligned with the Viksit Bharat@2047 vision, the strategy focuses on empowering rural communities, scaling proven development models, and strengthening grassroots institutions such as Self-Help Groups and Farmer Producer Organisations

items.title: Delhi High Court seeks Centre’s stand on plea to broadcast FIFA World Cup
items.summary: No broadcaster has acquired the broadcasting rights this year for the FIFA World Cup, one of the most viewed sporting events in the world, the petition says

items.title: India-Oman FTA on track for June 1 start even as deal with Chile hits hurdles, says Commerce Minister
items.summary: The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement was signed in December 2025

items.title: Southwest monsoon likely over Bay of Bengal and Andaman and Nicobar by week-end: IMD
items.summary: A low-pressure area persisting over the southwest Bay of Bengal is expected to strengthen over the next 48 hours, potentially aiding the monsoon’s progress

items.title: EXIM Bank plans $10.5 billion debt fundraise in fiscal 2027
items.summary: It will raise about $3.5 billion from overseas markets ⁠via bilateral and syndicated loans

items.title: US wheat harvest set to hit lowest level since 1972 amid severe Plains drought
items.summary: Wheat output for the 2026/27 season is estimated at 1.561 billion bushels, well below last year’s levels and market expectations.

items.title: Adani Ports to invest $1.36 billion in Europe expansion
items.summary: As ⁠part of the planned expansion, its marine services unit Astra Offshore will develop ​a fleet ‌of 200 vessels with specialised offshore capabilities

items.title: India set for record DAP fertiliser import in single tender amid supply concerns
items.summary: Indian Potash Ltd agreed to buy the cargoes at prices between $930 and $935 per tonne on a cost-and-freight basis for deliveries to both the west and east coasts

items.title: The toll of structural adjustments on the global South and a case for accountability
items.summary: In the 1980s, IMF and World Bank put conditions on financing the global South. Decades later, many countries in the regions struggle with weak public health systems and high levels of poverty. The institutions owe reparations for the damage caused, a new paper argues.

items.title: China moves WTO against India over solar cells, modules and tech incentives
items.summary: Beijing has alleged that India’s tariff structure and domestic preference policies discriminate against Chinese products and violate WTO agreements related to trade, subsidies and investment measures.

items.title: New Income Tax law aims to build transparent, tech-driven ecosystem for taxpayers: Official
items.summary: Pune's Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax, Vivek Wadekar, announced the new Income Tax Act, 2025, aims to simplify compliance and foster transparency. Launched on April 1, 2026, the act features digital initiatives like the 'Kar Saathi' chatbot and 'Kar Setu' for taxpayer assistance.

items.title: Kharif reset: Farmers may ditch paddy and maize for soybean, pulses amid monsoon fears
items.summary: As the kharif season approaches, farmers are leaning towards cultivating oilseeds like soybean and pulses such as urad and masur, primarily due to worries about erratic monsoon patterns and looming fertilizer shortages.
